details: The lower house of India's bicameral Parliament. It is composed of representatives of the people chosen by direct election on the basis of adult suffrage.
importance: Crucial for understanding legislative representation and the source of major national laws.

details: The upper house of India's bicameral Parliament. It is a permanent body and not subject to dissolution. One third of its members retire every second year.
importance: Key for understanding state representation at the center and review of legislation passed by Lok Sabha.
